cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: Some people complains about audio issue, I never encounter any. There are some polygons popping out of nowhere when an objects enters the view but I am not sure if this is due to the game or Wine
title: Foxhole

notes: Performance is slightly worse than on Windows; had to use winecfg and override xaudio_2_7 to native,builtin in order to fix audio issues (crackling in menu and lack of positional audio in game).
title: Foxhole

notes: Performance seems to be worse on Linux, I had to go to low settings to get smooth FPS even though my setup should run it on highest settings.
title: Foxhole

notes: You'll need a recent Proton version, otherwise it won't work.
title: Foxhole

notes:
Using AMD Radeon HD 6990M, starting the game would yield "DX11 feature level 10.0 is required to run the engine". Using Wine D3d11 seems to have fixed this.

Everything seems to run smoothly, except for the colors. The color palette will change from bright green to purple to all white (can still see names above players). This seems to depend on where the character is. Moving past certain boundaries will make the colors change, often significantly. The menus and maps all look normal. Playing in windowed, windowed fullscreen, or fullscreen doesn't seem to affect the problem. Tried messing with graphics settings but nothing seemed to help.
title: Foxhole

notes: Absolutely no issues that I can remember after playing this game for close to 100 hours.
title: Foxhole

notes:
I tested everything I can and it all works well without tweaks. I feel there is a small performance tax compared to running it on Windows, but that could be various other non-attributable factors. Do not use the WINE D3D11 tweak as one review has specified as it would cause your game to go into full laggy disco-mode (it will flash random palettes onto textures.) Just run it without any tweaks, and it works just fine. 

In the main menu, sometimes it doesn't register my left mouse button, but I cannot confirm whether or not that is due to Proton running the game. Any glitches I've encountered were also glitches that would occur if you ran the game natively on Windows.
title: Foxhole

notes: Need to enable Wine D3d11 to even start up, but after loading in there will be many texture issues and lighting issues, most notably with the water.
title: Foxhole
